steps.title: Identify the general pattern established in the first sentence
steps.content: The narrator opens with a broad observation: in boats, each crew member believes he is doing all the work. This is a general trend the narrator has noticed repeatedly.

steps.title: Match the underlined sentence's role to this pattern
steps.content:
The underlined sentence presents Harris's specific belief—that he alone worked while George and the narrator imposed on him—which is a concrete instance of the trend just announced. Answer B captures this structure precisely: the underlined sentence offers Harris's belief as a specific example of the broader pattern the narrator has observed. The other choices either misidentify what Harris's belief is about (A focuses on "how boat trips usually proceed" and C invokes "helping the group," neither of which Harris's belief concerns), overlook the role of the general observation in framing it, or contradict his firm, confident tone (D suggests confusion).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
